fr.elements: Main Building; Roof System; Landscaped Area; Pedestrian Walkways; Open Green Space
fr.recommendations: Improving the side access of the property by creating additional or widened access routes could enhance emergency responder entry and movement. Reviewing the density of vegetation near critical pathways and access points may facilitate better visibility and navigation for arriving responders. Ensuring that pedestrian walkways are clearly marked and unobstructed would support efficient responder movement throughout the property and aid in resource staging. Maintaining a clear perimeter around the main building and removing unnecessary obstructions could support effective emergency operations. Furthermore, strategically managing the open green space to allow for potential equipment staging and emergency vehicle positioning may increase operational flexibility and efficiency.

wildfire.conditions: Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Scattered Trees; Scattered Shrubs; Vegetated Perimeter; Open Green Space; Limited Defensible Space
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space around the property could involve managing the existing vegetation adjacent to structures to reduce potential fuel load. Increasing the separation between scattered trees and shrubs may decrease fuel continuity and help prevent fire spread. Maintaining and potentially expanding the vegetated perimeter into a more managed state could improve defensible space by creating a buffer that minimizes fire intensity near structures. Open green spaces offer a valuable fire spread deterrent, and their preservation can support long-term wildfire resilience. Additionally, careful vegetation management in these areas would bolster suppression efforts and offer firefighters a safer access and operational environment.
